title: ZUNION
description: Returns the union of multiple sorted sets.
syntax:
ZUNION numkeys key [key ...] [WEIGHTS weight [weight ...]]
  [AGGREGATE <SUM | MIN | MAX>] [WITHSCORES]
complexity: O(N)+O(M*log(M)) with N being the sum of the sizes of the input sorted sets, and M being the number of elements in the resulting sorted set.

redis_py.signature: zunion(, keys: Union[Sequence[KeyT], Mapping[AnyKeyT, float]],, aggregate: Optional[str] = None,, withscores: bool = False,, score_cast_func: Union[type, Callable] = float,)

returns.description: A set with members of the resulting set

redis_rb.signature: zunion(*keys, weights: nil, aggregate: nil, with_scores: false)

content:
This command is similar to [`ZUNIONSTORE`](), but instead of storing the resulting sorted set, it is returned to the client. For a description of the `WEIGHTS` and `AGGREGATE` options, see [`ZUNIONSTORE`](). ## Examples {{% redis-cli %}} ZADD zset1 1 "one" ZADD zset1 2 "two" ZADD zset2 1 "one" ZADD zset2 2 "two" ZADD zset2 3 "three" ZUNION 2 zset1 zset2 ZUNION 2 zset1 zset2 WITHSCORES {{% /redis-cli %}} ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | | ## Return information {{< multitabs id="zunion-return-info" tab1="RESP2" tab2="RESP3" >}} [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): the result of the union with, optionally, their scores when _WITHSCORES_ is used. -tab-sep- [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): the result of the union with, optionally, their scores when _WITHSCORES_ is used. {{< /multitabs >}}
example:
ZADD zset1 1 "one"
ZADD zset1 2 "two"
ZADD zset2 1 "one"
ZADD zset2 2 "two"
ZADD zset2 3 "three"
ZUNION 2 zset1 zset2
ZUNION 2 zset1 zset2 WITHSCORES
intro:
This command is similar to [`ZUNIONSTORE`](), but instead of storing the resulting
sorted set, it is returned to the client.

For a description of the `WEIGHTS` and `AGGREGATE` options, see [`ZUNIONSTORE`]().
notes: This command's behavior varies in clustered Redis environments. See the [multi-key operations]() page for more information.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
This command is similar to [`ZUNIONSTORE`](), but instead of storing the resulting
sorted set, it is returned to the client.

For a description of the `WEIGHTS` and `AGGREGATE` options, see [`ZUNIONSTORE`]().

sections.title: Examples
sections.content:
{{% redis-cli %}}
ZADD zset1 1 "one"
ZADD zset1 2 "two"
ZADD zset2 1 "one"
ZADD zset2 2 "two"
ZADD zset2 3 "three"
ZUNION 2 zset1 zset2
ZUNION 2 zset1 zset2 WITHSCORES
{{% /redis-cli %}}
